name.es: Rollo polaco de semillas de amapola (Makowiec)

description.en: A tight spiral of sweet dough and poppyseed filling, sliced like a loaf, that fills Polish homes at Christmas and Easter. Poppy seeds stand for prosperity, so this rolled bread is as much a good-luck charm as a treat.
description.de: Ein straff gerolltes Hefegebäck mit süßer Mohnfüllung, das an Weihnachten und Ostern in polnischen Küchen duftet. Mohn steht für Wohlstand - dieses gerollte Gebäck ist ebenso Glücksbringer wie Süßspeise.
description.es: Un rollo apretado de masa dulce con relleno de semillas de amapola, que perfuma los hogares polacos en Navidad y Pascua. Las semillas de amapola simbolizan la prosperidad, así que este pan enrollado es tanto amuleto como golosina.
description.fr: Un roulé serré de pâte sucrée et de garniture au pavot, tranché comme un pain, qui parfume les maisons polonaises à Noël et à Pâques. Le pavot symbolise la prospérité : ce roulé est autant un porte-bonheur qu'une gourmandise.

origin.note: Close kin to babka and stollen; poppy seeds are a traditional symbol of wealth and abundance for the year ahead, especially at the Christmas Eve supper (Wigilia).

ingredients.note: soaked in boiling water, then drained and finely ground

keeping.note: Improves overnight as the filling settles; slice thin once fully cooled so the spiral holds together.

method.title: Soak and grind the poppy seeds
method.summary: Cover the poppy seeds in boiling water and let them soak several hours or overnight, then drain well and grind fine in a food processor or spice grinder.

method.title: Build the filling
method.summary: Stir the ground poppy seeds with sugar, honey, melted butter, egg whites and raisins into a thick, spreadable paste.

method.title: Make the dough
method.summary: Warm the milk with the dough's sugar and butter, whisk in the yeast, then mix with the egg yolks, flour and salt into a soft dough; knead until smooth.

method.title: First rise
method.summary: Cover and let the dough rise until roughly doubled, about 2 hours.

method.title: Roll and fill
method.summary: Roll the dough into a large, thin rectangle and spread the poppyseed filling edge to edge, leaving a small border.

method.title: Roll into a spiral
method.summary: Roll up tightly from the long side into a log, pinch the seam shut, and set it seam-down on a lined sheet.

method.title: Second rise and bake
method.summary: Proof for about 35-40 minutes, brush with beaten egg, and bake at 180°C for around 40 minutes until deeply browned.

method.title: Cool and glaze
method.summary: Cool completely before slicing so the swirl holds its shape, then finish with a thin powdered-sugar glaze if you like.

source.adaptation: sugarPct, fatPct and hydration include the filling's sugar, honey, butter and egg whites, not just the dough's own - the filling carries most of the sweetness in this rolled loaf.
